{{Quote|John Wagner is one of the best comic book format storytellers in the business and a total professional. ''[…]'' John is really good at action stories. He knows how to build suspense, setting up scenes so when you turn a page, there's a payoff. ''[…]'' It's more than just putting words in balloons; you have to visually direct a scene.|[[Ryder Windham]]|<ref name="secrets">''[[The Secrets of Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire]]''</ref>}}
'''John Wagner''' (born [[July 18]], [[1940s#1949|1949]]) is a freelance comic book writer.

==Biography==
He is notable for co-creating [[Wikipedia:Judge Dredd|Judge Dredd]] for [[Wikipedia:2000 AD (comics)|''2000 AD'']] and for penning the graphic novel [[Wikipedia:A History of Violence (comics)|''A History of Violence'']].

He has written several ''[[Star Wars]]'' comic books for [[Dark Horse Comics]]. Working on ''Star Wars'' titles, Wagner considers that the characters are well-defined and is easy to give them the proper role.<ref name="secrets" />

[[Ryder Windham]] admired Wagner's work, especially his [[Wikipedia:Batman/Judge Dredd: Judgment on Gotham|''Batman/Judge Dredd: Judgment on Gotham'']] crossover, and hand-picked him for the [[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (comic series)|''Shadows of the Empire'' comic series]]. Wagner doesn't prefer working with ready synopses, since the story tends to "get stale", and the ''Shadows'' script begun as an outline by [[Steve Perry]]; however for him it grew and evolved thanks to changes coming back from [[Lucasfilm]].<ref name="secrets" />

===Personal life===
In the 1990s Wagner was living in a Tudor village outside London. The house he resided was built during the time of [[Wikipedia:Henry VIII|Henry VIII]] (also haunted, according to some stories), and it featured in some of his comics.<ref name="secrets" />
